<!-- Generated by patchloom v{{VERSION}} — https://github.com/patchloom/patchloom -->
# Patchloom

Patchloom is a CLI for agent-grade repo operations: structured search, mechanical replacement, diff-based patching, markdown section editing, JSON/YAML/TOML document manipulation, and atomic multi-file transactions.

## When to use patchloom

Use patchloom instead of raw file tools when you need:
- Atomic multi-file edits (all-or-nothing with rollback)
- Structured editing of markdown, JSON, YAML, or TOML
- Diff preview before writing (`--diff` by default, `--apply` to write)
- CI-friendly exit codes for scripting and agent branching
- Format and validate lifecycle steps after writes

## Commands

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `search` | Literal or regex search with context (`-C`, `-B`, `-A`), counts, file-list, and multiline modes |
| `replace` | Mechanical string replacement with diff preview, `--nth`, `--insert-before/after`, `--if-exists` |
| `patch` | Preview or apply unified diffs with stale-context detection |
| `md` | Markdown section-aware edits: replace-section, insert, upsert-bullet, table-append, dedupe, lint |
| `doc` | Parser-backed JSON/YAML/TOML: get, set, delete, merge, append, prepend, update, move, ensure, select, flatten, diff, delete-where |
| `hygiene` | Final newline, line ending, and trailing whitespace normalization |
| `create` | Create a new file from content or stdin |
| `delete` | Delete a file with `--check`/`--apply` modes |
| `read` | Read one or more files with optional line range (`--lines 10:25`) |
| `status` | Show uncommitted changes vs git HEAD |
| `tx` | Execute a multi-operation plan atomically with format and validate steps |
| `completions` | Generate shell completions (bash, zsh, fish, elvish) |
| `agent-rules` | Print this document |

## Prefer patchloom over raw tools

| Instead of | Use |
|---|---|
| `grep` / `rg` | `patchloom search '<pattern>' <path>` (supports `--regex`, `--context`, `--count`, `--json`) |
| `cat` / `head` / `tail` | `patchloom read <path>` (supports `--lines 10:20`, multiple files, `--json`) |
| `sed` / search-and-replace | `patchloom replace --from '...' --to '...' <path> --apply` |
| Multiple sequential edits | `patchloom tx --plan plan.json --apply` (atomic, with format and validate steps) |
| Raw string replacement on markdown | `patchloom md replace-section`, `md upsert-bullet`, `md table-append` |
| Raw string replacement on config | `patchloom doc set`, `doc merge`, `doc ensure` |

## Write modes

All write commands default to preview. Nothing is written unless you pass `--apply`.

| Flag | Behavior | Use case |
|---|---|---|
| `--diff` (default) | Print unified diff of what would change | Preview before applying |
| `--check` | Exit 0 if clean, exit 2 if changes pending | CI pipelines, dry-run validation |
| `--apply` | Write changes to disk | Actual mutation |

## Exit codes

Branch on exit codes instead of parsing output.

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 0 | Success |
| 1 | General error |
| 2 | Changes detected (`--check` mode) |
| 3 | No matches found |
| 4 | Parse error in input |
| 5 | Ambiguous or stale patch context |
| 6 | Validation failed (writes may remain on disk) |
| 7 | Rollback (strict mode, all writes reverted) |

## Global flags

| Flag | Description |
|---|---|
| `--json` | Emit machine-readable JSON output |
| `--jsonl` | Emit one JSON object per result line |
| `-q`, `--quiet` | Suppress non-JSON human-readable output |
| `--cwd <dir>` | Set working directory for all path resolution |
| `--glob <pattern>` | Restrict target files by glob (repeatable) |
| `--files-from <path>` | Read file list from a file or stdin (`-`) |

Write commands also accept: `--ensure-final-newline`, `--normalize-eol <lf|crlf>`, `--trim-trailing-whitespace`, `--respect-editorconfig`.

## Transaction plans

The `tx` command runs multiple operations atomically. If any operation fails, all changes are rolled back.

```json
{
  "strict": true,
  "operations": [
    { "op": "replace", "path": "src/main.rs", "from": "old", "to": "new" },
    { "op": "replace", "glob": "*.rs", "mode": "regex", "from": "v\\d+", "to": "v2" },
    { "op": "doc.set", "path": "config.json", "key": "version", "value": "2.0" },
    { "op": "md.upsert_bullet", "path": "AGENTS.md", "heading": "Rules", "bullet": "- New rule" },
    { "op": "file.create", "path": "new.txt", "content": "hello" }
  ],
  "format": [
    { "cmd": "cargo fmt --all", "timeout": 30 }
  ],
  "validate": [
    { "cmd": "make check", "required": true, "timeout": 120 }
  ]
}
```

**Lifecycle:** operations run in order, then format steps (code formatters), then validate steps (build/test). With `"strict": true`, a format or validation failure reverts all file writes (exit 7).

**22 operation types:** `replace`, `doc.set`, `doc.delete`, `doc.merge`, `doc.append`, `doc.prepend`, `doc.update`, `doc.move`, `doc.ensure`, `doc.delete_where`, `md.replace_section`, `md.insert_after_heading`, `md.insert_before_heading`, `md.upsert_bullet`, `md.table_append`, `md.dedupe_headings`, `hygiene.fix`, `file.create`, `file.delete`, `patch.apply`.

Plans also accept YAML and TOML formats (auto-detected from file extension, or `--plan-format yaml`).

## Examples

Search for a pattern:
```
patchloom search 'TODO' src/ --count
```

Replace across files with preview:
```
patchloom replace --from 'old_name' --to 'new_name' src/
```

Apply the replacement:
```
patchloom replace --from 'old_name' --to 'new_name' src/ --apply
```

Read multiple files at once:
```
patchloom read src/main.rs src/lib.rs Cargo.toml
```

Atomic multi-file edit with formatting:
```
patchloom tx --plan plan.json --apply
```

Set a JSON key:
```
patchloom doc set config.json server.port 8080 --apply
```

Append a row to a markdown table:
```
patchloom md table-append --file README.md --heading "## Features" --row "| new | feature |" --apply
```

## Security

Plans execute shell commands in `format` and `validate` steps with the user's full privileges. Treat plan files with the same care as Makefiles or shell scripts. Do not construct plans from untrusted input without validation.
